what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record for the design-source channel, declaring an images attachment that targets image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-19 and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-19
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
file has no body content beyond frontmatter
standouts: References a specific pitch image asset (p-19) under recent-pitches
Acts as a lightweight, timestamped link between channel memory and an image asset
conventions: Identifier follows an att- prefix pattern
Uses a channel-memory attachment schema with channel, attachment_type, and target fields
Target uses an image:// URI scheme for asset addressing
concerns: Target URI cannot be verified from this file alone; no checksum, size, or mime type for the image
No caption or author metadata to explain why the image is attached
notes: A metadata stub that attaches image asset p-19 to the design-source channel with a timestamp.
questions: What visual content does image p-19 contain?
Who added this attachment and for what specific purpose?
Is the target image currently available and versioned?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records an image attachment for a channel memory entry.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to catalog or link image assets associated with recent design pitches so they can be retrieved within the design-source channel context.
contribution: Provides a durable, timestamped pointer from a channel memory entry to a specific image asset, supporting retrieval and audit of design pitch visuals.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file linking the design-source channel to the image asset image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-19 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record that an image asset was attached to the design-source channel in reference to a recent pitch.
